Remark: Applicable for 2022, 2023 and 2024 batches students. & There maybe an interview or SOP process to select the students of the courseThis course will contain projects that involve hands-on application of materials science and chemistry to real world problem statements, especially involving sustainable technologies such as green energy materials, recycling, and sustainable manufacturing. Depending on the faculty offering the course, projects may range from dye sensitized solar cells, quantum dots, wearable electronics, non-destructive sensor development for structural integrity assessment, recycling of E-waste, textile, packaging material, making feedstock for 3D printing, lifecycle assessment and recycling of structural alloys. These will be carried out in groups of 2-3, with a maximum cap of 20 students per semester. Interdisciplinary participation is highly encouraged.
